text: This paper describes the Stanford CoreNLP toolkit, a Java annotated library that provides a suite of applications for linguistic analysis of the English language. The applications in the pipeline perform a variety of tasks including tokenization and sentences splitting, part-of-speech tagging, fully automatic tagging, named entity recognition, parsing (constituent and dependency), and coreference resolution. CoreNLP is a very flexible and efficient system, allowing users to easily apply a large number of text analysis technologies to large corpora. The system was designed to be consistent in architecture and to have a simple API for extracting linguistic features from text in an easy way. Currently, the toolkit has become a widely used tool in both research and practice, which is supported by the modular design that allows easy extension with new applications and for processing of other languages.
